---
title: "Compressing a cmdstanr funnel posterior"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Compressing a cmdstanr funnel posterior}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = "#>",
  fig.width  = 7,
  fig.height = 4
)
```

This vignette compresses Neal's funnel posterior (a classic stress test
because of the strong dependency between `y` and `x`) with `compress_fit()`,
then regenerates samples and inspects the approximation.

It requires a working CmdStan install.

```{r libs}
library(poco)
```

## Fit the funnel model

```{r fit_funnel}
library(cmdstanr)

stan_code <- "
data { int<lower=1> D; }
parameters {
  real y;
  vector[D] x;
}
model {
  y ~ normal(0, 3);
  x ~ normal(0, exp(y / 2));
}
"
mod <- cmdstan_model(write_stan_file(stan_code))

D <- 6L
fit <- mod$sample(
  data           = list(D = D),
  chains         = 4,
  parallel_chains = 4,
  iter_warmup    = 1000,
  iter_sampling  = 1000,
  refresh        = 0
)
```

## Compress

```{r compress}
vars <- c("y", paste0("x[", 1:D, "]"))
comp <- compress_fit(
  fit,
  variables    = vars,
  method       = "mclust",
  n_components = 5,
  model_name   = "VVV"
)
comp
```

You can also pass the CSV files directly:

```{r compress_csv}
comp <- compress_fit(
  fit$output_files(),
  variables    = vars,
  method       = "mclust",
  n_components = 5
)
```

## Regenerate samples and compare

```{r regenerate}
draws_orig <- fit$draws(variables = vars, format = "matrix")
draws_new  <- sample_posterior(comp, n_draws = nrow(draws_orig))

round(rbind(
  original     = colMeans(draws_orig),
  regenerated  = colMeans(draws_new)
), 3)
```

```{r plots, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

stacked <- rbind(
  data.frame(draws_orig[, c("y", "x[1]")], source = "original",
             check.names = FALSE),
  data.frame(draws_new [, c("y", "x[1]")], source = "regenerated",
             check.names = FALSE)
)

p1 <- ggplot(stacked, aes(y, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.35) + theme_minimal()
p2 <- ggplot(stacked, aes(y, `x[1]`, color = source)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.4) + theme_minimal()
patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, ncol = 2)
```

## Session info

```{r si, eval = TRUE}
sessionInfo()
```
